title: primula axillaris
intro: Primula axillaris, also known as the alpine primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the primrose family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Primula axillaris has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote digestive health, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function.
